2026-04-21T23:49:04.3559027Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T23:49:04.3559456Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T23:49:04.3559834Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T23:49:04.3560361Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T23:49:04.3560606Z [36;1m[0m

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Search.Documents.Indexes;\nusing Azure.Search.Documents.Indexes.Models;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.Search.Documents.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public partial class Sample13_IndexAlias : SearchTestBase\n    {\n        public Sample13_IndexAlias(bool async, SearchClientOptions.ServiceVersion serviceVersion)\n            : base(async, serviceVersion, null /* RecordedTestMode.Record /* to re-record */)\n        {\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        public async Task CreateAndQueryWithAlias()\n        {\n            await using SearchResources resources = await SearchResources.GetWithHotelsIndexAsync(this);\n            SearchIndexClient indexClient = resources.GetIndexClient();\n            string aliasName = Recording.Random.GetName();\n\n            #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_CreateAlias\n            // Create an alias pointing to an existing index\n            var alias = new SearchAlias(aliasName, new[] { resources.IndexName });\n            await indexClient.CreateOrUpdateAliasAsync(aliasName, alias);\n            #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_CreateAlias\n\n            #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_SearchWithAlias\n            // Query via the alias name instead of the index name directly\n            SearchClient aliasClient = resources.GetClient(aliasName);\n            SearchResults\u003cSearchDocument\u003e results = await aliasClient.SearchAsync\u003cSearchDocument\u003e(\"*\");\n            await foreach (SearchResult\u003cSearchDocument\u003e result in results.GetResultsAsync())\n            {\n                SearchDocument doc = result.Document;\n            }\n            #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_SearchWithAlias\n\n            #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_DeleteAlias\n            await indexClient.DeleteAliasAsync(aliasName);\n            #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_DeleteAlias\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### 2b — Image vector search sample test file\n\nCreate `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample14_ImageVectorSearch.cs` using `VectorizableImageUrlQuery` and `VectorizableImageBinaryQuery`. Use the same pattern as `Sample07_VectorSearch_UsingVectorizableTextQuery.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Search.Documents.Models;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.Search.Documents.Tests.Samples\n{\n    [PlaybackOnly(\"Requires a vectorizer-enabled index\")]\n    public partial class Sample14_ImageVectorSearch : SearchTestBase\n    {\n        public Sample14_ImageVectorSearch(bool async, SearchClientOptions.ServiceVersion serviceVersion)\n            : base(async, serviceVersion, null /* RecordedTestMode.Record /* to re-record */)\n        {\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        public async Task SearchWithImageUrl()\n        {\n            // Assumes an index with a vector field configured with an image vectorizer\n            await using SearchResources resources = SearchResources.CreateWithNoIndexes(this);\n            SearchClient client = resources.GetClient();\n\n            #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageUrlQuery\n            // Search using an image URL — the service vectorizes the image automatically\n            SearchResults\u003cSearchDocument\u003e results = await client.SearchAsync\u003cSearchDocument\u003e(\n                new SearchOptions\n                {\n                    VectorSearch = new VectorSearchOptions\n                    {\n                        Queries =\n                        {\n                            new VectorizableImageUrlQuery(new Uri(\"https://example.com/image.jpg\"))\n                            {\n                                KNearestNeighborsCount = 3,\n                                Fields = { \"imageVector\" }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                });\n            await foreach (SearchResult\u003cSearchDocument\u003e result in results.GetResultsAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine(result.Document[\"id\"]);\n            }\n            #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageUrlQuery\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        public async Task SearchWithImageBinary()\n        {\n            await using SearchResources resources = SearchResources.CreateWithNoIndexes(this);\n            SearchClient client = resources.GetClient();\n\n            #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageBinaryQuery\n            // Search using raw image bytes (base-64 encoded internally by the SDK)\n            byte[] imageBytes = System.IO.File.ReadAllBytes(\"path/to/image.png\");\n            SearchResults\u003cSearchDocument\u003e results = await client.SearchAsync\u003cSearchDocument\u003e(\n                new SearchOptions\n                {\n                    VectorSearch = new VectorSearchOptions\n                    {\n                        Queries =\n                        {\n                            new VectorizableImageBinaryQuery(imageBytes)\n                            {\n                                KNearestNeighborsCount = 3,\n                                Fields = { \"imageVector\" }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                });\n            await foreach (SearchResult\u003cSearchDocument\u003e result in results.GetResultsAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine(result.Document[\"id\"]);\n            }\n            #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageBinaryQuery\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### 2c — README placeholder additions\n\n**File:** The service must have a vectorizer configured on the target vector field.\n\n   **Search by image URL:**\n\n   ```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageUrlQuery\n   ```\n\n   **Search by image bytes:**\n\n   ```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample14_VectorizableImageBinaryQuery\n   ```\n   ```\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all six required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) in the correct order. 2026-04-21T23:52:26.4566583Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4566685Z **Package:** `Azure.Search.Documents`
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4566833Z **Service directory:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/`
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4567088Z **Triggered by:** commit 1942c3c4915e0b59408880080b3cee01f20673e8 (PR #58180) by @efrainretana
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4567098Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4567181Z ## What Changed
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4567185Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4568563Z PR #58180 updated the README for `Azure.Search.Documents` to cover 12.0.0 features, including knowledge base retrieval, DI/IConfiguration support, `SearchAlias`, and image-based vector search. The update added links and prose descriptions for `SearchAlias` and `VectorizableImageBinaryQuery`/`VectorizableImageUrlQuery` but did not add working code examples backed by test snippets for these two features.
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4568573Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4568647Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4568652Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4568947Z - No sample test file or markdown doc demonstrating `SearchAlias` (index alias create/get/delete lifecycle)
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4569351Z - No sample test file or markdown doc showing image-based vector search with `VectorizableImageBinaryQuery` and `VectorizableImageUrlQuery`
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4569769Z - The README Vector Search section links to the existing `Sample07_VectorSearch.md` which covers text/vectorized queries but omits image queries
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570155Z - The README Key Concepts `SearchAlias` bullet points to external REST docs; no inline C# code example exists anywhere in the samples directory
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570160Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570235Z <details>
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570450Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570455Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570763Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570767Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570849Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4570854Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571022Z No existing files need structural edits. New files must be created.
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571026Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571120Z ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571123Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571269Z #### 2a — Index Alias sample test file
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4571273Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572177Z Create `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample13_IndexAlias.cs` with the following structure (adapt based on existing test infrastructure — use `SearchResources`, `SearchTestBase`, `Recording` as in `Sample01_HelloWorld.cs`):
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572182Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572257Z ```csharp
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572411Z // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572504Z // Licensed under the MIT License.
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572508Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572604Z using System.Threading.Tasks;
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572704Z using Azure.Search.Documents.Indexes;
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572826Z using Azure.Search.Documents.Indexes.Models;
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572908Z using NUnit.Framework;
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4572912Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4573040Z namespace Azure.Search.Documents.Tests.Samples
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4573114Z {
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4573408Z     public partial class Sample13_IndexAlias : SearchTestBase
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4573596Z     {
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4573885Z         public Sample13_IndexAlias(bool async, SearchClientOptions.ServiceVersion serviceVersion)
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574123Z             : base(async, serviceVersion, null /* RecordedTestMode.Record /* to re-record */)
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574195Z         {
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574265Z         }
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574269Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574342Z         [Test]
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574462Z         public async Task CreateAndQueryWithAlias()
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574531Z         {
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574819Z             await using SearchResources resources = await SearchResources.GetWithHotelsIndexAsync(this);
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4574988Z             SearchIndexClient indexClient = resources.GetIndexClient();
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575118Z             string aliasName = Recording.Random.GetName();
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575122Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575326Z             #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_CreateAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575460Z             // Create an alias pointing to an existing index
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575643Z             var alias = new SearchAlias(aliasName, new[] { resources.IndexName });
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4575824Z             await indexClient.CreateOrUpdateAliasAsync(aliasName, alias);
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576030Z             #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_CreateAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576035Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576246Z             #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_SearchWithAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576399Z             // Query via the alias name instead of the index name directly
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576562Z             SearchClient aliasClient = resources.GetClient(aliasName);
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4576840Z             SearchResults<SearchDocument> results = await aliasClient.SearchAsync<SearchDocument>("*");
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577074Z             await foreach (SearchResult<SearchDocument> result in results.GetResultsAsync())
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577182Z             {
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577302Z                 SearchDocument doc = result.Document;
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577488Z             }
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577712Z             #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_SearchWithAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577716Z 
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4577909Z             #region Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_DeleteAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578039Z             await indexClient.DeleteAliasAsync(aliasName);
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578237Z             #endregion Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample13_DeleteAlias
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578307Z         }
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578383Z     }
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578452Z }
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578528Z ```
2026-04-21T23:52:26.4578532Z
